searchDocs.title: General​
searchDocs.content: What is Quantix?  Quantix is a stat modifier library for Roblox Luau. It manages numeric and table-based stats through a typed, phase-ordered pipeline. Modifiers are grouped by type (FlatAdd, Multiply, Override, etc.), evaluated in a fixed order, and cached until invalidated. Stats stack exactly as intended regardless of how many modifiers are active.    Is Quantix free?  Yes. Quantix is released under the MIT License.    What problems does Quantix solve?  Manual stat stacking with scattered + and * operations leads to order-of-operations bugs, duplicate logic, and no single source of truth. Quantix centralises all modifier logic: one Get call returns the correct final value every time.    Can I use Quantix on both server and client?  Yes. Quantix has no service dependencies beyond Signal. Require it on the server, the client, or both.

searchDocs.title: Modifiers​
searchDocs.content: What is the evaluation order?  Phases run in this fixed order: SetBase → FlatAdd → AddPercent → Multiply → Override → MinOverride → MaxOverride → Clamp → Lock. Behavior phases follow: BehaviorReplace → BehaviorOverride → BehaviorDeepMerge → BehaviorHook → BehaviorExclusive.    What is the difference between FlatAdd and AddPercent?  FlatAdd adds a constant amount (result + value). AddPercent adds a percentage of the base value, not the current result (result + base × (percent / 100)). This means AddPercent is not affected by prior FlatAdd mods.    What is the difference between Override and Lock?  Override replaces the result at its phase in the pipeline; subsequent phases (Clamp, Lock) still run. Lock short-circuits the entire pipeline immediately; no other phase applies. Use Lock when a stat must be a specific value regardless of anything else (e.g. a stunned character who always deals 0 damage).    How does Multiply work with multiple mods?  Ungrouped Multiply mods are summed and applied as result × (1 + sum). So two ungrouped mods of 0.1 each give result × 1.2. They are additive with each other, not compounding. To get compounding multiplication, assign mods to different MultGroup values; groups multiply together.    What does StackUnique do?  StackUnique is like FlatAdd but only the highest value per source contributes. It prevents the same source from stacking a bonus with itself while still allowing different sources to add their own bonus.    What is BehaviorReplace?  It sets a priority cutoff for behavior merging. Any BehaviorOverride, BehaviorDeepMerge, or BehaviorHook modifier with a priority lower than the active BehaviorReplace cutoff is ignored. Use it when an ammo type or attachment should completely replace the base behavior rather than patch it.

searchDocs.title: Evaluation & Caching​
searchDocs.content: Are stat values cached?  Yes. Get caches the final result for each stat after the first evaluation and returns the cached value on subsequent calls. The cache is invalidated whenever a modifier is added, removed, or the base value changes. Stats with a Condition function are never cached because their value depends on runtime state.    When does OnStatChanged fire?  After any modifier add/remove or SetBase call that results in a different final value. Inside a Batch, signals are deferred until EndBatch. One signal fires per stat that changed, not one per modifier.

searchDocs.title: Sources​
searchDocs.content: What is Source and SourceId for?  They tag modifiers so they can be removed in bulk. Source is a category (e.g. "Attachment", "Perk") and SourceId is the specific instance (e.g. the attachment's ID). RemoveBySource("Attachment", id) removes all modifiers applied by that specific attachment in one call.    Do I have to use the built-in source constants?  No. Quantix.Sources constants are provided for convenience, but Source is a plain string. Pass any value that makes sense for your project.

searchDocs.title: Behaviors​
searchDocs.content: What is the difference between BehaviorOverride and BehaviorDeepMerge?  BehaviorOverride does a shallow merge. Top-level keys in mod.Value overwrite the corresponding keys in the behavior table. BehaviorDeepMerge recurses into nested tables, so it can patch a specific sub-key without wiping its siblings.    How do BehaviorHook functions get called?  Evaluate returns (finalBehavior, hooks). The hooks table is { [eventName] = { fn, fn, ... } }. Your system is responsible for iterating and calling them at the appropriate lifecycle moment (e.g. OnPierce, OnImpact).

searchDocs.title: Lifecycle​
searchDocs.content: How do I clean up a StatController?  Call stats:Destroy(). It disconnects all signals, clears all internal tables, and makes the controller unusable. Always call Destroy when the owner (e.g. a weapon or character) is removed.    What happens if I call Get on a stat with no base value?  A warning is emitted and nil is returned. Register all stats via Quantix.new(data), SetBase, or RegisterBehavior before adding modifiers.

searchDocs.title: Weapon Stats with Attachments​
searchDocs.content: Create a controller from weapon data, apply attachment modifiers on equip, and remove them on unequip.  local Quantix = require(ReplicatedStorage.Quantix) local stats = Quantix.new({ Damage = 25, Range = 50, FireRate = 600, Spread = { Base = 1.5, ADS = 0.8 }, }) local function onAttachmentEquipped(attachment) stats:Batch(function() for _, mod in attachment.Modifiers do mod.Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment mod.SourceId = attachment.Id stats:AddModifier(mod) end end) end local function onAttachmentUnequipped(attachment) stats:RemoveBySource(Quantix.Sources.Attachment, attachment.Id) end

searchDocs.title: Perk Bonuses (StackUnique)​
searchDocs.content: A perk that gives +5 damage, but equipping the same perk twice should not double-stack it.  -- Only the highest value per source contributes stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = Quantix.Types.StackUnique, Value = 5, Source = Quantix.Sources.Perk, SourceId = "DamageBoost", }) -- Adding the same source again replaces rather than stacks stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = Quantix.Types.StackUnique, Value = 5, Source = Quantix.Sources.Perk, SourceId = "DamageBoost", }) print(stats:Get("Damage")) --> 30 (not 35)

searchDocs.title: Conditional Modifier (Rage Buff)​
searchDocs.content: A bonus that only applies while the character has an active buff.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = Quantix.Types.FlatAdd, Value = 15, Source = Quantix.Sources.State, Condition = function() return character:HasBuff("Rage") end, }) -- No cache is kept for conditional stats, always re-evaluated print(stats:Get("Damage")) -- includes +15 only when Rage is active

searchDocs.title: Locking a Stat (Stun)​
searchDocs.content: Force a stat to a fixed value regardless of all other modifiers.  local lockId = stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = Quantix.Types.Lock, Value = 0, Priority = 99, Source = Quantix.Sources.State, }) print(stats:Get("Damage")) --> 0 -- Remove the lock when stun ends stats:RemoveModifier(lockId)

searchDocs.title: Replacing Attachment Modifiers​
searchDocs.content: Swap one attachment for another atomically. No intermediate state fires.  local function swapAttachment(old, new) local newMods = {} for _, mod in new.Modifiers do mod.Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment mod.SourceId = new.Id table.insert(newMods, mod) end -- Removes old, adds new, fires signals once per changed stat stats:ReplaceBySource(Quantix.Sources.Attachment, old.Id, newMods) end

searchDocs.title: Ammo Type: Behavior Override​
searchDocs.content: Switch bullet behavior when a special ammo type is loaded, then restore defaults on unload.  stats:RegisterBehavior("Bullet", { MaxPenetrations = 1, Gravity = 9.81, Damage = 25, }) local function onAmmoLoaded(ammo) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = Quantix.Types.BehaviorOverride, Value = ammo.BehaviorOverrides, -- e.g. { MaxPenetrations = 3, Gravity = 0 } Source = Quantix.Sources.Ammo, SourceId = ammo.Id, Priority = 1, }) end local function onAmmoUnloaded(ammo) stats:RemoveBySource(Quantix.Sources.Ammo, ammo.Id) end local behavior, hooks = stats:Evaluate("Bullet", {})

searchDocs.title: Scope: BehaviorExclusive​
searchDocs.content: Only one scope type should apply at a time. BehaviorExclusive with a shared Tag guarantees the highest-priority one wins.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = Quantix.Types.BehaviorExclusive, Tag = "Scope", Value = { ZoomFactor = 2, AimSpeed = 0.8 }, Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment, SourceId = "IronSights", Priority = 0, }) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = Quantix.Types.BehaviorExclusive, Tag = "Scope", Value = { ZoomFactor = 4, AimSpeed = 0.6 }, Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment, SourceId = "4xScope", Priority = 1, }) -- Only 4xScope applies — it has the higher priority local behavior, _ = stats:Evaluate("Bullet", {}) print(behavior.ZoomFactor) --> 4

searchDocs.title: Batch Replace on Loadout Change​
searchDocs.content: Rebuild all perk modifiers at once after a loadout change, coalescing all signals into one pass.  local function onLoadoutChanged(newPerks) local mods = {} for _, perk in newPerks do for _, mod in perk:GetModifiers() do mod.Source = Quantix.Sources.Perk table.insert(mods, mod) end end stats:ReplaceBySource(Quantix.Sources.Perk, nil, mods) end

searchDocs.title: Debug Trace​
searchDocs.content: Inspect the full modifier pipeline for a specific stat during development.  print(stats:Trace("Damage")) -- Stat: Damage -- Base: 25 -- Active modifiers: 3 -- [FlatAdd] Attachment/scope value=0 priority=0 -- [AddPercent] Perk/DmgBoost value=15 priority=0 -- [Multiply] State/rage value=0.2 priority=0 -- Phase [FlatAdd]: -- FlatAdd: { sum=0 } -- Phase [AddPercent]: -- AddPercent: { totalPercent=15 } -- Phase [Multiply]: -- Multiply: { ungroupedSum=0.2, groups={} } -- = Final: 33.75

searchDocs.title: Types​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix.Types: {[string]: string} Table of all registered modifier type name constants. Key Value SetBase "SetBase" FlatAdd "FlatAdd" AddPercent "AddPercent" Multiply "Multiply" Override "Override" MinOverride "MinOverride" MaxOverride "MaxOverride" ClampMin "ClampMin" ClampMax "ClampMax" StackUnique "StackUnique" Lock "Lock" BehaviorReplace "BehaviorReplace" BehaviorOverride "BehaviorOverride" BehaviorDeepMerge "BehaviorDeepMerge" BehaviorHook "BehaviorHook" BehaviorExclusive "BehaviorExclusive" stats:AddModifier({ ..., Type = Quantix.Types.FlatAdd })

searchDocs.title: Sources​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix.Sources: {[string]: string} Built-in source category constants for tagging modifiers. Key Value Attachment "Attachment" Ammo "Ammo" Perk "Perk" State "State" System "System" Ballistics "Ballistics" stats:AddModifier({ ..., Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment, SourceId = id }) stats:RemoveBySource(Quantix.Sources.Attachment, id)

searchDocs.title: Signals​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix.Signals: StatControllerSignals Lifecycle signals for this controller. Signal Parameters Fires when OnStatChanged statName: string, newValue: any, oldValue: any A stat's final value changed OnModifierAdded modifier: Modifier A modifier was added OnModifierRemoved modifierId: string A modifier was removed OnBatchEnd (none) A Batch completed stats.Signals.OnStatChanged:Connect(function(statName, newValue, oldValue) print(statName, oldValue, "→", newValue) end)

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix.new( data: table-- Weapon/entity data table. Pass {} to register stats manually. ) → StatController Creates a new StatController. Numeric leaf values in data are flattened into dot-separated stat keys. -- Nested table → flat keys local stats = Quantix.new({ Damage = 25, Spread = { Base = 1.5, ADS = 0.8 }, }) -- stats:Get("Spread.Base") → 1.5 -- Manual registration local stats = Quantix.new({}) stats:SetBase("Damage", 25)

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:Get( statName: string-- The stat key to evaluate. ) → any-- The final value, or nil if the stat has no base. Returns the final evaluated value of a stat. Result is cached until the stat is invalidated. Conditional stats are always re-evaluated. print(stats:Get("Damage")) --> 35 print(stats:Get("Spread.Base")) --> 1.5

searchDocs.title: GetBase​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:GetBase(statName: string) → any Returns the raw base value of a stat before any modifiers are applied. print(stats:GetBase("Damage")) --> 25

searchDocs.title: GetAll​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:GetAll() → {[string]: any} Evaluates all registered stats and returns them as a { [statName]: value } table. local all = stats:GetAll() print(all["Damage"], all["Range"])

searchDocs.title: GetGroup​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:GetGroup( prefix: string-- The dot-path prefix to filter by. ) → {[string]: any} Returns all stats whose keys start with prefix., with the prefix stripped. -- Given stats: Spread.Base = 1.5, Spread.ADS = 0.8 local spread = stats:GetGroup("Spread") print(spread.Base, spread.ADS) --> 1.5 0.8

searchDocs.title: AddModifier​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:AddModifier( modifier: Modifier-- The modifier to add. Id is assigned automatically. ) → string-- The auto-generated modifier ID. Adds a modifier to the controller. Returns the modifier's auto-generated ID, which can be passed to RemoveModifier later. Required fields: Stat, Type, Value. local id = stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = Quantix.Types.FlatAdd, Value = 10, Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment, SourceId = attachment.Id, Priority = 0, })

searchDocs.title: RemoveModifier​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:RemoveModifier(modifierId: string) → boolean Removes a modifier by its ID. Returns true if the modifier was found and removed, false if it did not exist. local removed = stats:RemoveModifier(id)

searchDocs.title: RemoveBySource​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:RemoveBySource( source: string,-- The source category to match. sourceId: (string | number)?-- Optional specific source instance to match. ) → number-- The number of modifiers removed. Removes all modifiers with the given Source (and optionally SourceId). Runs inside an implicit Batch. Returns the number of modifiers removed. -- Remove all modifiers from any attachment with this ID stats:RemoveBySource(Quantix.Sources.Attachment, attachment.Id) -- Remove all Perk modifiers regardless of SourceId stats:RemoveBySource(Quantix.Sources.Perk)

searchDocs.title: ReplaceBySource​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:ReplaceBySource( source: string, sourceId: (string | number)?, newModifiers: {Modifier} ) → {string}-- IDs of the newly added modifiers. Removes all modifiers matching source/sourceId, then adds newModifiers atomically inside a single Batch. Returns a list of the new modifier IDs. local ids = stats:ReplaceBySource( Quantix.Sources.Attachment, old.Id, newAttachment.Modifiers )

searchDocs.title: ClearAll​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:ClearAll() → () Removes every active modifier. Runs inside an implicit Batch. stats:ClearAll()

searchDocs.title: SetBase​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:SetBase( statName: string, value: any-- New base value (number or table). ) → () Sets or updates the base value of a stat. Fires OnStatChanged if the final evaluated value changes. stats:SetBase("Damage", 30)

searchDocs.title: RegisterBehavior​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:RegisterBehavior( behaviorKey: string,-- Unique key for this behavior, used as the Stat field in modifiers. baseBehavior: table-- The unmodified base behavior definition. ) → () Registers a base behavior table under a key so that behavior modifiers can be applied to it via AddModifier. Call once during setup. stats:RegisterBehavior("Bullet", { MaxPenetrations = 1, Gravity = 9.81, })

searchDocs.title: Evaluate​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:Evaluate( behaviorKey: string,-- The behavior key (must be registered via RegisterBehavior). baseBehavior: table-- Fallback if no modifiers have been applied yet. ) → ( table,-- The final merged behavior table. table-- Hooks table: { [eventName] = { fn, ... } }. ) Evaluates behavior modifiers for behaviorKey and returns the merged behavior table and collected hooks. local behavior, hooks = stats:Evaluate("Bullet", baseBehavior) -- behavior: merged table (no _Hooks key) -- hooks: { [eventName] = { fn, fn, ... } } for _, fn in hooks.OnPierce or {} do fn(ctx, result, velocity) end

searchDocs.title: Batch​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:Batch() → () Runs fn inside a batch. All OnStatChanged, OnModifierAdded, and OnModifierRemoved signals are deferred until fn returns, then fired once per changed stat. OnBatchEnd fires last. stats:Batch(function() stats:RemoveBySource(Quantix.Sources.Attachment, id) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "FlatAdd", Value = 8, Source = "Attachment", SourceId = id }) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Range", Type = "Multiply", Value = 0.1, Source = "Attachment", SourceId = id }) end)

searchDocs.title: BeginBatch​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:BeginBatch() → () Manually begins a batch. Prefer Batch for automatic error handling. stats:BeginBatch() -- ... modifier changes ... stats:EndBatch()

searchDocs.title: EndBatch​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:EndBatch() → () Ends the active batch, flushing deferred signals.

searchDocs.title: GetModifiersForStat​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:GetModifiersForStat(statName: string) → {Modifier} Returns all active modifiers registered for statName. for _, mod in stats:GetModifiersForStat("Damage") do print(mod.Id, mod.Type, mod.Value) end

searchDocs.title: GetModifiers​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:GetModifiers() → {Modifier} Returns all active modifiers across all stats.

searchDocs.title: GetModifierById​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:GetModifierById(modifierId: string) → Modifier? Returns the modifier with the given ID, or nil if it does not exist.

searchDocs.title: Trace​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:Trace(statName: string) → string Returns a formatted multi-line string showing the full evaluation pipeline for statName: base value, all active modifiers, per-phase accumulators, and final result. Skipped (condition-failed) modifiers are listed separately. print(stats:Trace("Damage")) -- Stat: Damage -- Base: 25 -- Active modifiers: 2 -- [FlatAdd] Attachment/ value=10 priority=0 -- Phase [FlatAdd]: -- FlatAdd: { sum=10 } -- = Final: 35

searchDocs.title: DebugDump​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:DebugDump() → () Prints a full dump of all registered stats and active modifiers to the Roblox console. Useful for in-editor inspection. stats:DebugDump()

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Quantix:Destroy() → () Destroys the controller. Disconnects all signals and clears all internal state. Always call this when the owning entity (weapon, character) is removed. stats:Destroy()

searchDocs.title: Author​
searchDocs.content: Quantix was designed and written by the VeDevelopment team. The phase-ordered modifier pipeline, typed accumulator system, behavior modifier support, source-tagged bulk removal, and batched signal coalescing are original Luau implementation work.  Find us:  Discord ServerDirect MessageInstagramX / TwitterTikTok

searchDocs.title: Dependencies​
searchDocs.content: Quantix uses Signal for its lifecycle signal system (OnStatChanged, OnModifierAdded, OnModifierRemoved, OnBatchEnd), also developed by VeDevelopment.

searchDocs.title: Installation​
searchDocs.content: Get Quantix from the Roblox Creator Store:  Get Quantix on Creator Store  Drop the module into ReplicatedStorage and require it:  local Quantix = require(ReplicatedStorage.Quantix)   Quantix depends on Signal and ModifierTypes, both included in the package.

searchDocs.title: Your First StatController​
searchDocs.content: local Quantix = require(ReplicatedStorage.Quantix) -- Pass a weapon data table; numeric leaf values become stat keys local stats = Quantix.new({ Damage = 25, Range = 50, Spread = { Base = 1.5, ADS = 0.8 }, }) -- Read the base value print(stats:Get("Damage")) --> 25 print(stats:Get("Spread.Base")) --> 1.5

searchDocs.title: Adding Modifiers​
searchDocs.content: -- +10 flat damage from an attachment local id = stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = Quantix.Types.FlatAdd, Value = 10, Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment, }) print(stats:Get("Damage")) --> 35 -- Remove it later stats:RemoveModifier(id) print(stats:Get("Damage")) --> 25

searchDocs.title: Reacting to Changes​
searchDocs.content: StatController fires signals whenever a stat's final value changes:  stats.Signals.OnStatChanged:Connect(function(statName, newValue, oldValue) print(statName, oldValue, "→", newValue) end) stats.Signals.OnModifierAdded:Connect(function(mod) print("Modifier added:", mod.Id, mod.Type) end)

searchDocs.title: Batching Multiple Changes​
searchDocs.content: Wrap bulk modifier changes in a Batch to coalesce signals. One OnStatChanged fires per stat, not one per modifier:  stats:Batch(function() stats:RemoveBySource(Quantix.Sources.Attachment, attachmentId) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = Quantix.Types.FlatAdd, Value = 5, Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment, SourceId = attachmentId }) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Range", Type = Quantix.Types.Multiply, Value = 0.1, Source = Quantix.Sources.Attachment, SourceId = attachmentId }) end)

searchDocs.title: Behavior Modifiers​
searchDocs.content: Stats can also be tables. Use RegisterBehavior to set a base behavior, then modify it with BehaviorOverride, BehaviorDeepMerge, BehaviorHook, or BehaviorExclusive:  stats:RegisterBehavior("Bullet", { MaxPenetrations = 1, Gravity = 9.81, }) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = Quantix.Types.BehaviorOverride, Value = { MaxPenetrations = 3 }, Source = Quantix.Sources.Ammo, }) local behavior, hooks = stats:Evaluate("Bullet", {}) print(behavior.MaxPenetrations) --> 3

searchDocs.title: Quick Reference​
searchDocs.content: I want to…	MethodCreate a controller	Quantix.new Read a stat	stats:Get Read all stats	stats:GetAll Read a group of stats	stats:GetGroup Add a modifier	stats:AddModifier Remove a modifier	stats:RemoveModifier Remove all from a source	stats:RemoveBySource Replace source modifiers	stats:ReplaceBySource Batch multiple changes	stats:Batch Debug a stat pipeline	stats:Trace See practical examples	Use Cases

searchDocs.title: One File. One Require.​
searchDocs.content: Drop Quantix into ReplicatedStorage and require it from any script.  local Quantix = require(ReplicatedStorage.Quantix)

searchDocs.title: Creating a StatController​
searchDocs.content: local stats = Quantix.new({ Damage = 25, Range = 50, Spread = { Base = 1.5, ADS = 0.8 }, }) -- Nested tables are flattened to dot-path keys print(stats:Get("Spread.Base")) --> 1.5 print(stats:Get("Spread.ADS")) --> 0.8   Pass an empty table if you prefer to register stats manually:  local stats = Quantix.new({}) stats:SetBase("Damage", 25)

searchDocs.title: Modifier Pipeline​
searchDocs.content: Modifiers are evaluated in a fixed phase order. Within a phase, each type has its own accumulation and application logic.  Phase	Types	What it doesSetBase	SetBase	Replaces the base value (highest priority wins) FlatAdd	FlatAdd, StackUnique	Adds a flat amount to the result AddPercent	AddPercent	Adds base × (percent / 100) Multiply	Multiply	Multiplies the result; supports groups Override	Override	Replaces the result (highest priority wins) MinOverride	MinOverride	Raises the floor MaxOverride	MaxOverride	Lowers the ceiling Clamp	ClampMin, ClampMax	Final min/max clamp Lock	Lock	Short-circuits everything; final value is locked BehaviorReplace	BehaviorReplace	Sets a priority cutoff for behavior merging BehaviorOverride	BehaviorOverride	Shallow-merges keys onto the behavior table BehaviorDeepMerge	BehaviorDeepMerge	Recursively merges nested tables BehaviorHook	BehaviorHook	Collects hook functions per event name BehaviorExclusive	BehaviorExclusive	Tag-based winner-takes-all override

searchDocs.title: FlatAdd​
searchDocs.content: Adds a flat value after the base phase. All FlatAdd modifiers are summed.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "FlatAdd", Value = 10, Source = "Attachment" })

searchDocs.title: AddPercent​
searchDocs.content: Adds base × (percent / 100). All AddPercent modifiers are summed before applying.  -- +15% of base damage stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "AddPercent", Value = 15, Source = "Perk" })

searchDocs.title: Multiply​
searchDocs.content: Multiplies the post-additive result. Ungrouped multipliers are summed then applied as 1 + sum. Grouped multipliers multiply together per group.  -- Ungrouped: value is additive with other ungrouped Multiply mods stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "Multiply", Value = 0.2, Source = "Perk" }) -- Grouped: multiplied together within the group stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "Multiply", Value = 1.1, MultGroup = "WeaponBonus", Source = "State" })

searchDocs.title: Override​
searchDocs.content: Replaces the result entirely. Highest Priority wins when multiple Override mods exist.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "FireRate", Type = "Override", Value = 600, Priority = 10, Source = "State" })

searchDocs.title: SetBase​
searchDocs.content: Replaces the base value before any additive phases. Highest priority wins.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "SetBase", Value = 50, Priority = 1, Source = "System" })

searchDocs.title: MinOverride / MaxOverride​
searchDocs.content: Raises or lowers the result's floor or ceiling (applied after Multiply/Override).  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "MinOverride", Value = 1, Source = "System" }) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "MaxOverride", Value = 999, Source = "System" })

searchDocs.title: ClampMin / ClampMax​
searchDocs.content: Hard clamp applied last (after all other phases except Lock).  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Spread", Type = "ClampMin", Value = 0.1, Source = "System" })

searchDocs.title: StackUnique​
searchDocs.content: Like FlatAdd, but only the highest value per Source contributes. Prevents the same source from stacking with itself.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "StackUnique", Value = 5, Source = "Perk" })

searchDocs.title: Lock​
searchDocs.content: Short-circuits the entire pipeline. The highest-priority Lock mod's value is the final result, ignoring all other phases.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "Lock", Value = 0, Priority = 99, Source = "State" })

searchDocs.title: Behavior Modifier Types​
searchDocs.content: Behavior modifiers operate on a table base value registered via RegisterBehavior.

searchDocs.title: BehaviorOverride​
searchDocs.content: Shallow-merges mod.Value keys onto the behavior table. Higher priority wins per key.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = "BehaviorOverride", Value = { MaxPenetrations = 3, Gravity = 0 }, Source = "Ammo", })

searchDocs.title: BehaviorDeepMerge​
searchDocs.content: Recursively merges mod.Value into the behavior table. Safe for nested config tables.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = "BehaviorDeepMerge", Value = { MaterialRestitution = { Grass = 0.2 } }, Source = "Ammo", })

searchDocs.title: BehaviorHook​
searchDocs.content: Collects hook functions per event name. Multiple hooks for the same event are called in priority order.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = "BehaviorHook", Value = { OnPierce = function(ctx, result, velocity) velocity *= 0.8 end, }, Source = "Perk", })

searchDocs.title: BehaviorExclusive​
searchDocs.content: Tag-based winner-takes-all. Only the highest-priority mod per Tag applies. Prevents multiple conflicting mods from stacking (e.g. two different scope types).  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = "BehaviorExclusive", Tag = "Scope", Value = { ZoomFactor = 4 }, Source = "Attachment", Priority = 1, })

searchDocs.title: BehaviorReplace​
searchDocs.content: Sets a priority cutoff. Behavior modifiers with lower priority than the highest BehaviorReplace are ignored. Use to make an attachment fully override the base behavior.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Bullet", Type = "BehaviorReplace", Value = true, Priority = 5, Source = "Ammo", })

searchDocs.title: Signals​
searchDocs.content: Signal	Parameters	Fires whenOnStatChanged	statName, newValue, oldValue	A stat's final value changed OnModifierAdded	modifier	A modifier was added OnModifierRemoved	modifierId	A modifier was removed OnBatchEnd	(none)	A batch completed  stats.Signals.OnStatChanged:Connect(function(statName, newValue, oldValue) print(statName, oldValue, "→", newValue) end)

searchDocs.title: Batching​
searchDocs.content: Wrap multiple modifier changes in Batch to coalesce signals. OnStatChanged fires once per stat at the end, not once per modifier.  stats:Batch(function() stats:RemoveBySource("Attachment", id) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "FlatAdd", Value = 8, Source = "Attachment", SourceId = id }) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Range", Type = "Multiply", Value = 0.1, Source = "Attachment", SourceId = id }) end) -- OnStatChanged fires for "Damage" and "Range" once each

searchDocs.title: Source Tagging​
searchDocs.content: Every modifier can carry a Source and SourceId to group modifiers by what applied them. RemoveBySource removes all modifiers from a source in one call.  -- Apply all mods from an attachment stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", ..., Source = "Attachment", SourceId = attachment.Id }) stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Range", ..., Source = "Attachment", SourceId = attachment.Id }) -- Remove them all when the attachment is unequipped stats:RemoveBySource("Attachment", attachment.Id)   Built-in source constants are available as Quantix.Sources:  Constant	ValueAttachment	"Attachment" Ammo	"Ammo" Perk	"Perk" State	"State" System	"System" Ballistics	"Ballistics"

searchDocs.title: Conditional Modifiers​
searchDocs.content: Pass a Condition function to a modifier. It is called on every evaluation. If it returns false, the modifier is skipped. Conditional stats are never cached.  stats:AddModifier({ Stat = "Damage", Type = "FlatAdd", Value = 10, Source = "Perk", Condition = function() return character:HasBuff("Rage") end, })

searchDocs.title: Trace​
searchDocs.content: Returns a formatted string showing the full evaluation pipeline for a stat.  print(stats:Trace("Damage")) -- Stat: Damage -- Base: 25 -- Active modifiers: 2 -- [FlatAdd] Attachment/ value=10 priority=0 -- [Multiply] Perk/ value=0.2 priority=0 -- Phase [FlatAdd]: -- FlatAdd: { sum=10 } -- Phase [Multiply]: -- Multiply: { ungroupedSum=0.2, groups={} } -- = Final: 42

searchDocs.title: DebugDump​
searchDocs.content: Prints a full dump of all stats and active modifiers to the console.  stats:DebugDump()
